column4: Used either with an infinitive or a locative (of “reference,” viṣaya-saptamī).
Generally śakya- is used when the accompanying infinitive has a passive sense (e.g., vaktum · śakyam, “able to be said”), in contrast to vaktum · samarthaḥ “able to say.”

This is the more proximate version of tathā (“in that way, thusly”)
An indeclinable word that construes either with an instrumental (enough with...) or an infinitive (capable of...).
This word always comes in the second position in the sentence or clause.
